page: Amazon Web Services c6in.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It features 48 virtual CPUs mapped to 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It runs on the Nitro hypervisor and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the c6in.12xlarge in the average performance tier for most standard workloads, with a single-core stress-ng CPU score of 1893.0 and a multi-core score of 46470.96. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AES cryptography extensions, which contributes to a strong performance in the Passmark CPU encryption test, scoring 43676.13. Image processing benchmarks also show strong results at 15221.0. In contrast, memory bandwidth drops from 2506.71 GB/sec for cached small blocks to 163.17 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, highlighting a potential bottleneck for memory-intensive operations.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high network throughput, this server is configured for network-intensive compute workloads, such as distributed analytics, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference. It processed 2446.12 tokens per second in prompt processing for a small 135M LLM model, though performance drops significantly on larger models. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the lack of local storage and specialized GPU hardware means users pay primarily for dedicated compute cycles and high-speed network interfaces, making it a specialized choice for network-bound and CPU-bound tasks rather than memory-heavy databases.
description: Amazon Web Services c6in.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) and 96.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but offers a high baseline network bandwidth of 75 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard CPU and memory tasks, with strong results in image processing and Passmark CPU encryption tests. The processor supports AVX-512 and AES instruction sets. This configuration is suited for network-intensive compute workloads, static web serving, and software compilation, offering a balanced tradeoff between high network throughput and dedicated compute resources without the added cost of local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.12xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 48 vCPUs, 96 GB DDR4 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for network-intensive and CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6in.12xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 48 vCPUs, 96 GB RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring high-speed networking and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for network-bound and CPU-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by a 3.5 GHz Intel Xeon 8375C processor.
96.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included.
Strong performance in encryption and image processing benchmarks.
Suited for network-intensive compute, software compilation, and web serving.
